ROOT_001.title: The Great Overflow
ROOT_001.context: In 2026, WasteVille's only municipal landfill is at 85% of its permitted airspace. EPA MSW trend data shows food scraps and single-use plastics driving most of the growth. You have one year of runway.

POLICY_REDUCE_01.title: The Plastic Ban Bites
POLICY_REDUCE_01.text: Retailers file a lawsuit; low-income residents can't afford compostables.
POLICY_REDUCE_01.context: Single-use plastics grew from <1% of MSW in 1960 to ~13% today. A blanket ban cuts landfill inflow but stresses retailers and regressively hits low-income households without subsidies.

choices.text: Subsidize compostable packaging for SMBs and SNAP households.
Hold the line. Strict fines for non-compliance.

POLICY_PAYT_02.title: Pay-As-You-Throw Rollout
POLICY_PAYT_02.text: Weight-based billing cuts tonnage 18% — but illegal dumping appears near parks.
POLICY_PAYT_02.context: PAYT programs typically divert 15-30% of waste, but regressive without protections. Enforcement mechanisms carry equity costs.

choices.text: Install trash cameras in dumping hotspots.
Fund free community cleanup + bulk drop-off days.

INFRA_WTE_01.title: Waste-to-Energy's Ash Problem
INFRA_WTE_01.text: The WTE stacks are lit — but residents near the plant report respiratory issues.
INFRA_WTE_01.context: WTE reduces landfill volume ~90% but produces bottom/fly ash (10-25% of input by weight) that requires hazardous-class landfilling and stresses downwind air quality.

choices.text: Export ash to a neighboring state under regulated contracts.
Build a hazardous-ash landfill near the industrial flats.

STRICT_CRISIS_01.title: Status Quo Breakdown
STRICT_CRISIS_01.text: Regulators issue a Notice of Violation. Rats and odor complaints spike.
STRICT_CRISIS_01.context: Deferring structural action accelerates capacity exhaustion. Emergency hauling costs roughly 3-5x contracted rates.

choices.text: Sign emergency hauling contracts at premium rates.
Delay and issue compliance notices only.

CRISIS_GATE_01.title: The Equity Threshold
CRISIS_GATE_01.text: Neighborhood councils request an audience. Route will be chosen by the state of the city.
CRISIS_GATE_01.context: Routing gate. If SocialEquity<30 or PublicSentiment<30, a NIMBY protest branch triggers; otherwise Phase 1 stabilization.

choices.text: Hold a town hall and hear the next demand.

PHASE1_STABILIZE_01.title: Phase 1 Stabilized
PHASE1_STABILIZE_01.text: The city absorbed the shock. Citizens are cautiously hopeful.
PHASE1_STABILIZE_01.context: Route to Action Center stabilized guidance.

PHASE1_FAILURE_01.title: Phase 1 Failed
PHASE1_FAILURE_01.text: The city fractures along equity lines. The mayor resigns.
PHASE1_FAILURE_01.context: Route to Action Center failure guidance (equity-centered mitigation).

LANDFILL_FIRE.title: CATASTROPHE: Landfill Fire
LANDFILL_FIRE.text: Methane pockets ignited at the overflowing landfill. Smoke blots out the sun.
LANDFILL_FIRE.context: Triggered when InfrastructureLoad exceeds 80. Subsurface landfill fires can smolder for years and release dioxins. Real-world precedent: Bridgeton, MO (2010-ongoing).

choices.text: Emergency foam + evacuation of the downwind school district.
Let it burn out. Issue shelter-in-place and save the budget.

MARKET_CRASH.title: CATASTROPHE: Recyclables Market Crash
MARKET_CRASH.text: China's National Sword hits WasteVille. Mixed paper is suddenly worthless.
MARKET_CRASH.context: Triggered by a random 5% roll. Global commodity shocks (like the 2018 China National Sword policy) can collapse recyclable buyer markets overnight, forcing municipalities to landfill diverted material.

choices.text: Take an emergency bond to build a domestic MRF.
Landfill the diverted stream and cut the recycling program.
